page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, this server provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

The instance features 600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-speed local disk access directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Hardware-level cryptographic and vector acceleration are supported via instruction sets including AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, though AVX-512 extensions are not available on this processor model.

Performance evaluations place the c5ad.4xlarge primarily in the average tier across most compute and memory benchmarks. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations score in the middle 50% of tested systems. However, single-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 10% in stress-ng testing. Memory bandwidth also presents a bottleneck, with large-block read and write operations scoring in the bottom 25%, contrasting with average cached small-block read speeds. Large-model LLM prompt processing is similarly limited, though smaller model inference and static web serving maintain average throughput.

Given its compute-focused memory ratio and integrated NVMe storage, the c5ad.4xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring local scratch space and moderate parallel processing. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe SSDs provides qualitative cost efficiency for applications that can leverage local ephemeral storage to avoid network storage overhead. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations, though single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth are weak. The instance supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions but lacks AVX-512. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for applications that leverage local ephemeral storage to avoid network storage overhead. It is suited for workloads requiring local scratch space and parallel processing, such as web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded software compilation.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with high-speed local NVMe storage for parallel processing and scratch-space workloads.

32.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
